description: Figure 6 presents bainitic transformation kinetics measured by dilatometry for five low-density nano-bainitic Al–C steels, plotting relative length change (RLC, %) versus time. Panels (a) and (b) use logarithmic time axes to compare transformation behaviors: (a) shows high-Al steels S1–S3 (Al ≥ 6 wt.%) at austempering temperatures (T_B) of 270–350 °C, while (b) shows lower-Al steels S4–S5 (Al < 6 wt.%) under varying austenitizing (T_A: 1150–1250 °C) and austempering conditions. Panels (c)–(e) replot the same data on linear time scales to facilitate reading of transformation times, with vertical dashed lines marking the approximate bainite finish time (Bt) for each condition. The curves demonstrate that S3 (8.08 wt.% Al) exhibits the slowest transformation kinetics, requiring up to 43,000 s at 270 °C, whereas S4 (Ti-microalloyed) shows accelerated nucleation due to TiMoC precipitates but suppressed progression caused by bainite sheaf impingement. S5 transforms most rapidly, completing within approximately 700–1000 s.
